stic Data Consortium (LDC), a source of data and tools that support linguistic research (http://ldc.upenn.edu/). This was requested by Profs Wilensky and Klein in Spring 2005 for classes such as CS288, CS294-5 and CS298-13. Documentation is

s CS288, CS294-5 and CS298-13. Documentation is available under http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/pub/ (requires an EECS Instructional UNIX account to login). Each LDC package has an "index.html" file that describes the package. Instructio

html" file that describes the package. Instructions for reading the data are in http://www.ldc.upenn.edu/Using/ EECS Instruction funded the annual Not-for-Profit Standard membership for years 2005 and 2006. This includes up to 16 free "co

2005 and 2006. This includes up to 16 free "corpora" (data sets) each year from the catalog for the current year. Other corpora typically have an additional cost of between $100-$3000 each. (see http://www.ldc.upenn.edu/Catalog/). EECS

(see http://www.ldc.upenn.edu/Catalog/). EECS researchers may copy our corpora to their servers, and they may fund additional corpora. Please contact kevinm@eecs.berkeley.edu for help. The corpora are on a disk that is accessible as /hom

corpora are on a disk that is accessible as /home/tmp/LDC on Instructional UNIX and MacOSX computers \\ping\tmp\LDC on Instructional Windows computers We have installed these "corpora" (data sets) ---------- ---------------------------------
